title: Building a Content Engine with Next.js
excerpt: How to structure a modern editorial workflow with MDX, static generation, and an admin panel.
content:
# Why a Content Engine Matters
A consistent publishing rhythm compounds over time.

## The architecture
- Next.js App Router
- MDX content body
- JSON-backed admin workflows

### Rendering strategy
Use server components for fast page loads and cache where possible.

```ts
export function estimateCadence(postsPerWeek: number) {
  return postsPerWeek * 52;
}
```

![Editorial Board](https://picsum.photos/700/320?random=31)

## Final thought
Build the system once, then spend energy on ideas, not tooling.

title: From Draft to Published: A Clean Workflow
excerpt: Practical checkpoints to keep your writing quality high while shipping weekly.
content:
# Drafting with clarity
Your first draft should optimize for speed.

## Editing checklist
1. Improve headline promise
2. Tighten intro and transitions
3. Add one actionable framework

```md
### Rule of thumb
If a paragraph does not move the argument forward, trim it.
```

## Visual support
![Workflow diagram](https://picsum.photos/700/320?random=32)

### Wrap-up
A simple process beats an ambitious but inconsistent one.

title: Designing for Readability in Tech Blogs
excerpt: Typography, spacing, and information hierarchy decisions that increase completion rate.
content:
# Readability first
Readers scan before they commit.

## Three layout wins
- Keep paragraph widths comfortable
- Use meaningful subheadings
- Add visual anchors every 2-3 sections

![Readable layout](https://picsum.photos/700/320?random=33)

## Conclusion
Great writing deserves great presentation.

title: Analytics for Small Content Teams
excerpt: A tiny metrics stack that helps you choose what to publish next.
content:
# Measure what matters
Vanity metrics can distract decision-making.

## Core KPIs
- Time on page
- Scroll depth
- Returning visitors

```ts
const coreKpis = ["time_on_page", "scroll_depth", "return_rate"];
```

![Analytics board](https://picsum.photos/700/320?random=34)

### Action loop
Review weekly, decide monthly, optimize quarterly.

title: MDX Components for Better Storytelling
excerpt: Using custom components inside Markdown to create richer educational posts.
content:
# Beyond plain markdown
MDX enables interactive and visual storytelling blocks.

## Good use cases
- Callout notes
- Embedded charts
- Reusable checklists

```tsx
export function Callout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return <aside className="rounded-lg border p-4">{children}</aside>;
}
```

![Story components](https://picsum.photos/700/320?random=35)

### Key takeaway
Design reusable content primitives once, then use them everywhere.

title: Content Repurposing Playbook for Founders
excerpt: Turn one deep article into a week of multi-format social and newsletter content.
content:
# Repurpose with intent
One canonical post can fuel your full distribution week.

## Repurposing map
- Long-form article -> newsletter
- 3 takeaways -> social thread
- One framework -> carousel

```bash
# weekly content rhythm
write -> edit -> publish -> repurpose
```

![Repurposing flow](https://picsum.photos/700/320?random=36)

## Finish strong
Consistency compounds trust.
